title: Conservation Planning Datasets
text: prioritizrdata R package supplemental package contains example datasets conservation planning. intended used alongside prioritizr R package.

title: Conservation Planning Datasets
text: package contains following datasets. Tasmania dataset: Conservation planning dataset Tasmania, Australia. Salt Spring Island dataset: Conservation planning dataset Tasmania, Australia. Washington dataset: Conservation planning dataset Washington, United States.

title: Conservation Planning Datasets
text: latest official version prioritizrdata R package can installed using following R code. Alternatively, latest development version can installed using following code. Please note developmental versions may contain additional features present official version, may also contain coding errors.

title: Salt Spring Island data — salt_data
text: Conservation planning dataset Salt Spring Island, Canada. obtained part online Marxan-based planning tool created Coastal Douglas-fir Conservation Partnership (CDFCP; Schuster et al. 2017).

title: Salt Spring Island data — salt_data
text: salt_pu terra::rast() object. salt_features terra::rast() object. salt_con terra::rast() object.

title: Salt Spring Island data — salt_data
text: following functions provided import data: get_salt_pu() Import planning unit data. planning units  single layer terra::rast() object. Cell values denote monetary cost acquiring different areas (e.g., value 1 = $100,000 CAD; BC Land Assessment 2015). get_salt_features() Import biodiversity feature data. feature data multi-layer terra::rast() object object. contains spatial distribution four key ecological communities. layer represents different community type. classes () old forest, (ii) savanna, (iii) wetland, (iv) shrub. layer, values indicate composite probability encountering suite bird species commonly associated community type. get_salt_con() Import connectivity data. connectivity data single-layer terra::rast() object. contains inverse probability occurrence human commensal species. Based assumption human modified areas impede connectivity native fauna, cells higher values higher connectivity.

title: Salt Spring Island data — salt_data
text: BC Assessment (2015) Property Information Services. Available https://www.bcassessment.ca/ (Date Accessed 2016/06/13). Morrell N, Schuster R, Crombie M, Arcese P (2017) Prioritization Tool Conservation Coastal Douglas-fir Forest Savannah Habitats Georgia Basin. Nature Trust British Colombia, Coastal Douglas Fir Conservation Partnership, Department Forest Conservation Sciences, University British Colombia. Available https://peter-arcese-lab.sites.olt.ubc.ca/files/2016/09/CDFCP_tutorial_2017_05.pdf (Date Accessed 2017/10/09).

title: Salt Spring Island data — salt_data
code: # load packages library(terra) #> terra 1.9.27 library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.2, GDAL 3.9.3, PROJ 9.4.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  # import data salt_pu <- get_salt_pu() salt_features <- get_salt_features()  # preview planning units print(salt_pu) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 280, 200, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 100, 100  (x, y) #> extent      : 454589.9, 474589.9, 5394614, 5422614  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N (EPSG:32610) #> source      : salt_pu.tif #> name        :    cost #> min value   : 0.02552 #> max value   :   10000 plot(salt_pu)   # preview features print(salt_features) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 280, 200, 4  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 100, 100  (x, y) #> extent      : 454589.9, 474589.9, 5394614, 5422614  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N (EPSG:32610) #> source      : salt_features.tif #> names       : old forest,  savanna,  wetland,    shrub #> min values  :   0.359505, 0.297921, 0.113279,  0.40131 #> max values  :   0.931229, 0.660817, 0.643471, 0.824972 plot(salt_features)   # preview connectivity data salt_con <- get_salt_con() print(salt_con) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 280, 200, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 100, 100  (x, y) #> extent      : 454589.9, 474589.9, 5394614, 5422614  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N (EPSG:32610) #> source      : salt_con.tif #> name        : inverse human #> min value   :      0.370364 #> max value   :      0.903266 plot(salt_con)

title: Tasmania data — tas_data
text: Conservation planning dataset Tasmania, Australia.

title: Tasmania data — tas_data
text: tas_pu sf::st_sf() object. tas_features terra::rast() object

title: Tasmania data — tas_data
text: following functions provided import data: get_tas_pu Import planning unit data. planing units sf::st_sf() simple features object. row corresponds different planning unit, columns contain information planning units. columns contain: ("id") unique identifiers ("cost") unimproved land values planning units. also contains columns ("locked_in" "locked_out") logical values (.e. TRUE FALSE values) locking locking planning units. data obtained "Introduction Marxan" course originally generated part larger spatial prioritization Resources (Klein et al. 2007). get_tas_features Import biodiversity feature data. feature data multi-layer terra::rast() object. classes. layer corresponds different vegetation class contains binary cell values indicate presence absence vegetation class. data obtained Australian Government's National Vegetation Information System (Australian Government Department Climate Change, Energy, Environment Water 2020).

title: Tasmania data — tas_data
text: Klein C, Carwardine J, Wilson K, Watts M, Possingham H (2007) Spatial Prioritization Approaches Conservation Biodiversity Australia: Considering Conservation Costs, Ecological & Evolutionary Processes, Large-Intact Areas. Report Department Environment; Water Resources. Australian Government Department Climate Change, Energy, Environment Water (2020). National Vegetation Information System. Version 6.0. Available https://digital.atlas.gov.au/maps/national-vegetation-information-system-nvis-version-6-0-extant-vegetation.

title: Tasmania data — tas_data
code: # load packages library(terra) library(sf)  # load data tas_pu <- get_tas_pu() tas_features <- get_tas_features()  # preview planning units print(tas_pu) #> Simple feature collection with 1130 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 298809.6 ymin: 5167775 xmax: 613818.8 ymax: 5502544 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / UTM zone 55S #> # A tibble: 1,130 × 5 #>       id  cost locked_in locked_out                                         geom #>    <int> <dbl> <lgl>     <lgl>                                <MULTIPOLYGON [m]> #>  1     1 60.2  FALSE     TRUE       (((328497 5497704, 326783.8 5500050, 326775… #>  2     2 19.9  FALSE     FALSE      (((307121.6 5490487, 305344.4 5492917, 3053… #>  3     3 59.7  FALSE     TRUE       (((321726.1 5492382, 320111 5494593, 320127… #>  4     4 32.4  FALSE     FALSE      (((304314.5 5494324, 304342.2 5494287, 3043… #>  5     5 26.2  FALSE     FALSE      (((314958.5 5487057, 312336 5490646, 312339… #>  6     6 51.3  FALSE     FALSE      (((327904.3 5491218, 326594.6 5493012, 3284… #>  7     7 32.3  FALSE     FALSE      (((308194.1 5481729, 306601.2 5483908, 3066… #>  8     8 38.4  FALSE     FALSE      (((322792.7 5483624, 319965.3 5487497, 3199… #>  9     9  3.55 FALSE     FALSE      (((334896.6 5490731, 335610.4 5492490, 3357… #> 10    10  1.83 FALSE     FALSE      (((356377.1 5487952, 353903.1 5487635, 3538… #> # ℹ 1,120 more rows plot(tas_pu)   # plot features print(tas_features) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 398, 359, 33  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 1000, 1000  (x, y) #> extent      : 288801.7, 647801.7, 5142976, 5540976  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 55S (EPSG:32755) #> source      : tas_features.tif #> names       : Banks~lands, Bould~marks, Calli~lands, Cool ~orest, Eucal~hyll), Eucal~torey, ... #> min values  :           0,           0,           0,           0,           0,           0, ... #> max values  :           1,           1,           1,           1,           1,           1, ... plot(tas_features)

title: Washington data — wa_data
text: Conservation planning dataset Washington, United States America.

title: Washington data — wa_data
code: get_wa_pu()  get_wa_locked_in()  get_wa_locked_out()  get_wa_species()  get_wa_attr()  get_wa_carbon()

title: Washington data — wa_data
text: get_wa_pu terra::rast() object. get_wa_locked_in terra::rast() object. get_wa_locked_out terra::rast() object. get_wa_carbon terra::rast() object. get_wa_species terra::rast() object. get_wa_attr tibble::tibble() object.

title: Washington data — wa_data
text: following functions provided import data: get_wa_pu() Import planning unit data. planning units single layer terra::rast() object. Cell values denote land acquisition costs. data originally obtained Nolte (2020 ,b). get_wa_locked_in() Import locked data. locked data single layer terra::rast() object. Cell values denote binary values indicating cell predominantly covered protected areas (excluding mandate biodiversity protection). data originally obtained USGS (2022) get_wa_locked_in() Import locked data. locked data single layer terra::rast() object. Cell values denote binary values indicating cell predominantly covered urban areas. data originally obtained Commission Environmental Cooperation (2020) get_wa_carbon() Import vulnerable carbon data. carbon data single layer terra::rast() object. Cell values denote continuous values representing amount carbon sequestered vulnerable released typical land-use conversion. data originally obtained Noon et al. (2021, 2022) get_wa_species() Import species distribution data. feature data multi-layer terra::rast() object. contains spatial distribution 258 bird species. account migratory patterns, data provided breeding non-breeding distributions species (indicated "breeding" "non-breeding" layer names). species lacking information, species denoted full distribution (indicated "full" layer names). data originally obtained eBird Status Trends dataset (Fink et al. 2020). ensure backwards compatibility previous versions package, get_wa_features() can also used access data. get_wa_attr() Import attribute data species. feature attribute data data frame (tibble::tibble()) object. contains taxonomic information feature (.e., layer get_wa_species()) well estimates public interest (derived Mittermeier et al. 2021) extinction risk (based methodology Davis et al. 2018 threat status classification data IUCN 2025). Since Mittermeier et al. (2021) contain public interest scores features, scores interpolated features missing scores based average public interest score features belong taxonomic family. object following columns: feature Name feature (.e., per get_wa_species()). binomial Taxonomic species genus name feature. family Taxonomic family name feature. order Taxonomic order feature. extinction_prob Probability extinction. interest_score Public interest score.

title: Washington data — wa_data
text: Commission Environmental Cooperation. (2020). 2015 Land Cover North America 30 Meters. North American Land Change Monitoring System, 2nd Edition, https://www.cec.org:443/north-american-environmental-atlas/land-cover-30m-2015-landsat--rapideye/. Davis M, Faurby S, Svenning J-C (2018) Mammal diversity take millions years recover current biodiversity crisis. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 115: 11262–11267. Fink D, Auer T, Johnston , Ruiz-Gutierrez V, Hochachka WM Kelling S (2020) Modeling avian full annual cycle distribution population trends citizen science data. Ecological Applications, 30: e02056. IUCN (2025) IUCN Red List Threatened Species. Version 2025-2. https://www.iucnredlist.org. Accessed 13 May 2026. Mittermeier JC, Roll U, Matthews TJ, Correia R, Grenyer R (2021) Birds commonly encountered wild attract higher public interest online. Conservation Science Practice, 3: e340. Nolte C (2020a) Data : High-resolution land value maps reveal underestimation conservation costs United States. Dryad, Dataset, doi:10.5061/dryad.np5hqbzq9 . Nolte C (2020b) High-resolution land value maps reveal underestimation conservation costs United States. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 117: 29577–29583. Noon ML, Goldstein , Ledezma JC, Roehrdanz PR, Cook-Patton SC, Spawn-Lee SA, Wright TM, Gonzalez-Roglich M, Hole DG, Rockström J, Turner WR (2022) Mapping irrecoverable carbon Earth's ecosystems. Nature Sustainability, 5: 37–46. Noon ML, Goldstein , Ledezma JC, Roehrdanz PR, Cook-Patton SC, Spawn-Lee SA, Wright TM, Gonzalez-Roglich M, Hole DG, Rockström J, Turner WR (2021) Mapping irrecoverable carbon Earth's ecosystems (2.0) [Data set]. Zenodo. doi:10.5281/zenodo.4091029 . U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) Gap Analysis Project (GAP) (2022) Protected Areas Database United States (PAD-US) 3.0: U.S. Geological Survey data release, doi:10.5066/P9Q9LQ4B .

title: Washington data — wa_data
code: # load packages library(terra)  # import data wa_pu <- get_wa_pu() wa_species <- get_wa_species() wa_attr <- get_wa_attr() wa_locked_in <- get_wa_locked_in() wa_locked_out <- get_wa_locked_out() wa_carbon <- get_wa_carbon()  # preview planning units print(wa_pu) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 109, 147, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 4000, 4000  (x, y) #> extent      : -1816382, -1228382, 247483.5, 683483.5  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : +proj=laea +lat_0=45 +lon_0=-100 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=sphere +units=m +no_defs #> source      : wa_pu.tif #> name        :        cost #> min value   :    0.298665 #> max value   : 1804.183838 plot(wa_pu)  # preview locked in print(wa_locked_in) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 109, 147, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 4000, 4000  (x, y) #> extent      : -1816382, -1228382, 247483.5, 683483.5  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : +proj=laea +lat_0=45 +lon_0=-100 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=sphere +units=m +no_defs #> source      : wa_locked_in.tif #> name        : protected areas #> min value   :               0 #> max value   :               1 plot(wa_locked_in)   # preview locked out print(wa_locked_out) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 109, 147, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 4000, 4000  (x, y) #> extent      : -1816382, -1228382, 247483.5, 683483.5  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : +proj=laea +lat_0=45 +lon_0=-100 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=sphere +units=m +no_defs #> source      : wa_locked_out.tif #> name        : urban areas #> min value   :           0 #> max value   :           1 plot(wa_locked_out)   # preview species print(wa_species) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 109, 147, 396  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 4000, 4000  (x, y) #> extent      : -1816382, -1228382, 247483.5, 683483.5  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : +proj=laea +lat_0=45 +lon_0=-100 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=sphere +units=m +no_defs #> source      : wa_features.tif #> names       : Recur~ding), Botau~ding), Botau~ding), Corvu~ding), Corvu~ding), Cincl~full), ... #> min values  :           0,           0,           0,           0,           0,           0, ... #> max values  :       0.514,       0.812,       3.129,       0.115,       0.296,        0.06, ... plot(wa_species)   # preview attributes of species print(wa_attr) #> # A tibble: 396 × 6 #>    feature                  binomial family order extinction_prob interest_score #>    <chr>                    <chr>    <chr>  <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1 Recurvirostra americana… Recurvi… Recur… Char…          0.0009              0 #>  2 Botaurus lentiginosus (… Botauru… Ardei… Pele…          0.0009              1 #>  3 Botaurus lentiginosus (… Botauru… Ardei… Pele…          0.0009              1 #>  4 Corvus brachyrhynchos (… Corvus … Corvi… Pass…          0.0009              0 #>  5 Corvus brachyrhynchos (… Corvus … Corvi… Pass…          0.0009              0 #>  6 Cinclus mexicanus (full) Cinclus… Cincl… Pass…          0.0009              0 #>  7 Spinus tristis (breedin… Spinus … Fring… Pass…          0.0009              0 #>  8 Spinus tristis (nonbree… Spinus … Fring… Pass…          0.0009              0 #>  9 Falco sparverius (breed… Falco s… Falco… Falc…          0.0009           5281 #> 10 Falco sparverius (nonbr… Falco s… Falco… Falc…          0.0009           5281 #> # ℹ 386 more rows

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.3
text: Update Washington dataset additional data multi-objective optimization. particular, now contains data vulnerable carbon well feature weights derived public interest scores extinction probabilities. new data can accessed new get_wa_carbon() get_wa_weights() functions.

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.2.2
text: Update package manual entry.

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.2.1
text: Fix aliasing package manual entry (#14).

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.2
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-06 Release candidate CRAN. Update dependency versions. Fix README badges.

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.1
text: Fix documentation pass CRAN package checks.

title: prioritizrdata 0.3.0
text: Update package use sf terra R packages. Salt Spring Island Tasmania datasets updated can imported using new get_salt_pu(), get_salt_features(), get_salt_con(), get_tas_pu(), get_tas_features() functions. Salt Spring Island dataset now layer names features. Tasmania dataset updated feature data using NVIS. also contains layer names features. New Washington dataset added. can imported using get_wa_pu(), get_wa_features(), get_wa_locked_in() functions.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.4.2
text: Fix tests fail package checks given strict dependencies (#8). Update citation information README. Remove rgdal R package Suggests, longer needed (#10). Remove rgeos R package Suggests, due pending retirement (#10).

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.4.1
text: Implement GitHub Actions continuous integration (.e. update tests README).

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.4
text: CRAN release: 2020-08-05 Release candidate CRAN.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.3.2
text: Fix PROJ7 issues Tasmania Salt Spring Island dataset employing different coordinate reference system (#5). Rescale cost values Salt Spring Island planning units avoid numerical instability issues using data exact solvers. Update Tasmania feature data pixels NA values marine areas.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.3.1
text: Assorted documentation improvements (e.g. fixing typos broken URLs). Fix “Non-file package-anchored link(s) documentation object” warnings R-devel checks.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.3
text: Release candidate CRAN.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.2.1
text: Fix spelling coauthor first name.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.1.1
text: Depends R version 3.4.0 (avoids “patchlevel 0” NOTE/WARNING checks).

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.1 (released)
text: CRAN release: 2017-11-12 Release candidate CRAN.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.0.3
text: Fix broken links. Tutorials now compatible prioritizr version 3.0.1. Remove Remotes since prioritizr now CRAN.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.0.2
text: Fix spelling documentation. Update broken links. Lint R files. Make vignettes compatible new version prioritizr. Add prioritizr Remotes DESCRIPTION.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.0.1
text: Fix links README vignettes. Remove log files accidentally committed. Fix image size salt vignette.

title: prioritizrdata 0.2.0.0
text: Created package website. Add viridis, rasterVis, _prioritizr_packages Suggests used vignettes. Remove unused assertthat package Imports. Disable evaluation code chunk library() calls Saltspring.Rmd vignette can compiled lpsymphony, Rsymphony gurobi installed. Package authors now consistent prioritizr package. Simplify vignette workflow. Vignettes can now compiled using devtools::build_vignettes(). Earlier versions needed vignettes compiled using Makefile copy files around avoid tangled R code causing failures R CMD CHECK. Although longer needed, vignettes can still compiled using shell command make vigns desired. README.Rmd now lives top-level directory following standard practices. now complied using rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd") using shell command make readme. Note figures README.md can found directory man/figures.

title: prioritizrdata 0.1.1.0
text: Initial package version.
